content: GitHub API rate limiting: Primary rate limit is 5000 requests/hour for authenticated PAT requests. Secondary rate limits are more dangerous: triggered by making too many concurrent requests to resource-mutating endpoints (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) — GitHub returns HTTP 403 with 'You have exceeded a secondary rate limit' or HTTP 429. In CI test environments with real GitHub API calls, running the full test suite (say 50 tests, each creating a team or repo) will trigger secondary limits quickly. Prevention: (1) Never make real GitHub API calls in unit/integration tests — mock with respx. (2) If e2e tests need real GitHub, run them as a separate CI job with strict rate limiting and cleanup. (3) Add Retry-After header parsing to httpx client — GitHub returns this on 429/403 secondary. (4) Respect X-RateLimit-Remaining header and backoff when below threshold.
